question: ABCDEFGHIJ is a regular 10-gon with center M and circumradius 9. Let Q be the line through points I and F. Line X passes through points C and B. Let N be the line through point H parallel to line Q. Line Z passes through point C perpendicular to line X. Line T is the perpendicular bisector of segment AF. Line O is the reflection of line T across line Z. Lines O and N intersect at point Y. What is the distance between points Y and B? Express your answer as a decimal number rounded to 2 decimal places.

ABCDEFGHIJK is a regular 11-gon with center W and circumradius 1. Line X is the angle bisector of angle CKA. Point R is the midpoint of segment CG. Point Y is the reflection of point G across line X. What is the length of segment YR? Express your answer as a decimal number rounded to 2 decimal places.

ABCDEF is a regular hexagon with center V and circumradius 6. Point Y is the reflection of point F across segment CD. Let P be the midpoint of segment CD. Let J be the reflection of point Y across point V. What is the distance between points J and P? Express your answer as a decimal number rounded to 2 decimal places.

ABCD is a square with center P and circumradius 7. Point G is the midpoint of segment PB. Let N be the reflection of point B across point C. Point U is the midpoint of segment ND. What is the distance between points U and G? Express your answer as a decimal number rounded to 2 decimal places.

BCDEFGHIJK is a regular 10-gon with center A and circumradius 5. Let L be the rotation of point C by angle 3pi/4 around point F. Point Z is obtained by rotating point I by angle 5pi/12 about point K. Line V is the angle bisector of angle LZA. Point S is the reflection of point E across line V. What is the distance between points I and S? Express your answer as a decimal number rounded to 2 decimal places.

ABCD is a square with center T and circumradius 7. Let E be the perpendicular bisector of segment TC. Line I passes through points B and C. Line M passes through point A parallel to line E. Lines I and M intersect at point V. What is the distance between points T and V? Express your answer as a decimal number rounded to 2 decimal places.

Let ABCDFGHIJKLM be a regular dodecagon with center E and circumradius 5. Let O be the circumcircle of polygon ABCDFGHIJKLM. Point Q is on segment MI. Let T be the inversion of point Q in circle O. What is the measure of angle ITE, in radians? Express your answer as a decimal number rounded to 2 decimal places.

BCDEFG is a regular hexagon with center A and circumradius 2. Let Y be the circumcircle of polygon BCDEFG. Point H is obtained by rotating point D by angle pi/2 about point B. Let Z be the reflection of point H across point D. Circle I is the reflection of circle Y across point Z. Let K be the inversion of point Z in circle I. Find the distance between points K and F. Express your answer as a decimal number rounded to 2 decimal places.

ABCDEF is a regular hexagon with center M and circumradius 7. Let G be the midpoint of segment EF. Point H is obtained by rotating point D by angle 5pi/6 about point F. Let W be the circumcircle of polygon ABCDEF. Let X be the reflection of point G across point E. Point T is the inversion of point X with respect to circle W. Find the length of segment HT. Express your answer as a decimal number rounded to 2 decimal places.

ABCD is a square with center Z and circumradius 4. Let Q be the midpoint of segment AZ. Let P be the line through points Q and D. Line W passes through points A and B. Point G is the intersection of lines P and W. What is the distance between points C and G? Express your answer as a decimal number rounded to 2 decimal places.
